description: South Peace Caribou Resource Review Area The resource review areas (RRAs) established in 2012 as part of British Columbia's commitment to protect high elevation winter caribou habitat across the South Peace area to support the recovery of Northern Caribou have been revised. The Ministry of Natural Gas Development will not be accepting new petroleum and natural gas tenure requests in these areas. The revised RRAs are shown on the attached map and shapefile. This revision is based on improved habitat mapping that has been reviewed with local First Nations and industry. The range of Northern Caribou in British Columbia span from the Alberta border west to the coastal mountain ranges and north to the Yukon. There are approximately 17,000 Northern Caribou in British Columbia. Additional information can be found on the following websites:
lineage: http://www.newsroom.gov.bc.ca/2012/11/bc-government-commits-to-protect-northern-caribou-in-the-south-peace.html http://www.env.gov.bc.ca/wld/speciesconservation/nc/index.html Boreal Caribou Resource Review Area British Columbia has set aside over 500,000 hectares of identified boreal caribou habitat in "resource review areas (RRAs)" within which the province will not be accepting new natural gas and petroleum tenure requests for a minimum period of five years. The RRAs are shown on the attached maps and shapefile. Northeastern British Columbia encompasses the western extent of boreal caribou in Canada. This sub-species of 'woodland caribou' is listed as threatened in British Columbia and is a Priority 1 species for action under the BC Conservation Framework. The RRAs will be revisited after five years when the effectiveness of caribou population management measures and population assessments are better understood. Government will collaborate on this step with First Nations, industry and stakeholders. Additional information can be found on the attached Information Bulletin: http://www2.news.gov.bc.ca/news_releases_2009-2013/2010EMPR0027-000734.htm Elle Bardol (Elle.Bardol@gov.bc.ca) BCGOV MNGD - Petroleum and Natural Gas Titles Branch
